affected_programs.reason: Directly includes PAYMENT-RECORD copybook; contains GOTO statements (high risk); leaf program in payment processing chain
key_concerns: CRITICAL: Contains 2x GOTO statements (lines 50, 58) - control flow risk
Leaf program - final handler for payment transactions
LINKAGE SECTION uses PAYMENT-RECORD - interface contract change
Writes to PAYMENT-LOG file - audit trail concerns
Payment type routing logic (REGULAR/REFUND/REVERSAL) might be affected

affected_programs.reason: Directly includes PAYMENT-RECORD copybook; uses 3 copybooks total; central hub in processing chain
key_concerns: MEDIUM complexity - more extensive testing required
Calls PAYMENT-HANDLER with WS-PAYMENT-REQUEST - interface must remain compatible
Has REDEFINES clause - potential data overlay concerns

affected_programs.reason: Calls ACCOUNT-MGR which uses PAYMENT-RECORD; data flows through to payment processing
key_concerns: Must verify return codes from ACCOUNT-MGR still valid

affected_programs.reason: Entry point program; calls CUSTOMER-PROC which eventually leads to payment processing
key_concerns: Entry point - must verify end-to-end batch processing
Full regression test required after all changes complete

Apply PAYMENT-RECORD changes, update LINKAGE SECTION interface

dependencies: All programs updated and individually tested
recommended_change_sequence.validation: Full batch run with production-like data volume

recommended_change_sequence.description: Complete end-to-end testing with all payment scenarios
recommended_change_sequence.validation: Test all payment types, error conditions, and audit trails

risk_assessment.rationale: PAYMENT-RECORD is critical for payment processing. Changes affect payment transaction recording, routing logic, and audit trails.

critical_factors.description: Control flow complexity increases change risk significantly. MUST refactor before applying copybook changes.
COMP-3 packed decimal field - any precision/scale changes could cause financial errors
PAY-TYPE field has 88-level conditions controlling program flow - changes could misdirect payments
PAYMENT-LOG file writes must match updated structure - historical audit concerns
ACCOUNT-MGR uses 3 copybooks together - changes could affect business logic integration

mitigation_strategies.description: Eliminate GOTO statements in PAYMENT-HANDLER BEFORE applying PAYMENT-RECORD changes to reduce control flow complexity
If possible, add new fields without changing existing field positions/sizes
If record layout changes, plan conversion for PAYMENT-LOG historical data
No other changes to these 4 programs during the PAYMENT-RECORD modification window
Test all payment types: REGULAR, REFUND, REVERSAL with all status combinations

estimated_total_effort.note: CRITICAL: Must refactor GOTO statements first (adds 2 days to timeline)

tree: │   ├── PAYMENT-HANDLER (119 lines, MEDIUM complexity, 0 callees) ⚠️ HAS GOTO
│   │   └── Risk: CRITICAL - Contains GOTO statements + monetary data
└── BATCH-RUNNER (124 lines, LOW complexity) - Entry point

compliance_considerations.audit_trail: CRITICAL - PAYMENT-LOG file serves as audit trail for financial transactions. Any record layout changes must maintain audit integrity.
compliance_considerations.financial_accuracy: CRITICAL - PAY-AMOUNT is COMP-3 signed numeric. Precision must be maintained to avoid monetary calculation errors.
compliance_considerations.data_retention: All payment transactions must be retained per regulatory requirements. Plan data conversion if structure changes.
compliance_considerations.change_control: Payment processing changes require CAB approval and extended change window

tests: Test all status transitions (PENDING → APPROVED, etc.)
Test error handling with invalid payment data

scenarios: Customer with valid account processes payment
Customer with missing account error handling
Full batch processing with mixed transaction types

regression_tests: All existing payment processing test cases

key_actions: 1. CRITICAL: Refactor GOTO statements in PAYMENT-HANDLER FIRST (before copybook changes)
2. Freeze all other changes to payment processing programs
3. Create comprehensive test data covering all payment types and statuses
4. Plan extended change window (minimum 4 hours) with rollback capability
5. Notify all stakeholders - payment processing is critical business function
6. Prepare data conversion scripts if PAYMENT-LOG format changes
7. Schedule CAB review for this high-risk change

green_flags: ✅ Only 2 programs directly use this copybook
✅ No OCCURS or REDEFINES in PAYMENT-RECORD
red_flags: ⚠️ PAYMENT-HANDLER contains GOTO statements (MUST refactor first)
⚠️ ACCOUNT-MGR uses 3 copybooks together - complex interactions

recommendations.final_recommendation: PROCEED WITH HIGH CAUTION. This is a high-risk change affecting critical payment processing. MANDATORY prerequisite: Refactor GOTO statements in PAYMENT-HANDLER before applying copybook changes. Recommend 2-week timeline with extended testing phase.
